title: Allionia decumbens
taxonomy: Allionia decumbens is a species within the plant family Nyctaginaceae, commonly known as the four o'clock family. This family consists of around 30 genera and approximately 500 species. The genus Allionia contains a limited number of species, with Allionia decumbens being one of them. Allionia decumbens is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Allionia decumbens as a flowering plant is also reflected in its botanical characteristics, including its distinctive flowers and growth habits.
uses: Allionia decumbens, also known as trailing four o'clock, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in wound healing and reducing pain. In addition, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. However, the effectiveness and safety of Allionia decumbens as a medicinal agent have not been extensively scientifically studied, and more research is needed to confirm its potential uses. Some traditional medicine practitioners continue to use the plant as a treatment for various conditions.

intro: Allionia decumbens, also known as Lianthus or Allionia, is a perennial plant species native to the Americas, belonging to the Four O'Clock family (Nyctaginaceae). The health benefits of Allionia decumbens include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial agent, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Allionia decumbens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Allionia decumbens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
